title: Hybrid scorer replay control

queries: ml replay control experiment candidate rerun variance confirmation hybrid scorer
deep learning rerun confirmation structured control promising candidate replay

papers.title: N/A
papers.claim: This is a replay-control candidate rather than a literature-derived modeling idea.
papers.relevance: Used to determine whether the first simultaneous user-ltv and item-incoterms strict-baseline gains survive rerun variance.

evidence.why_now: EXP096 is the first post-control architecture result to push both user-ltv and item-incoterms above their strict baselines at the same time. Because the implemented scorer change only affects autocomplete multiclass tasks, the user-churn regression needs replay confirmation before the family is rejected.

hypothesis: Replaying the pairwise + autoregressive hybrid scorer under the same protocol will show whether the simultaneous user-ltv and item-incoterms gains survive rerun variance and whether the user-churn regression was a no-op drift rather than a stable global failure.
notes: Control only. This is an exact replay of EXP096 at the modeling level.
Do not mix new loss terms, sampling tweaks, or packaging changes into this replay.
Launched on 2026-05-07 through the local Stage 3 launcher.
First stable orchestrator status check shows all three windows up; user-churn and user-ltv are in Amazon DB-load startup while item-incoterms is in early salt startup.
Completed on 2026-05-07 with global verdict: promotable.
Outcome: replay survived on all three representative tasks, with user-churn, user-ltv, and item-incoterms all above their stored strict baselines.
